rationale: All three round-2 pins re-verify. (1) Status anchor: the new `^- \*\*Status\*\*: Accepted` regex matches the repo's bolded header on all three real Accepted ADRs I tested (0034/0037/0039); the Domain anchor `Domain\(s\).*\bcore\b.*\bworkflow\b` matches a real `core, workflow, execution` Domain line under the canonical /usr/bin/grep (BSD/GNU-compatible 2.6.0). (2) ReviewerCountNote wiring (Bead 4): both new key_file_paths resolve — internal/complete/panel_advisory.go and panel_advisory_test.go exist; `ReviewerCountNote` is correctly absent today (Bead 3 creates it); the seams panelScanFn/panelTallyFn/panelAdvisoryOut, the advisoryOut writer, the matched panelReg, and a loaded gateCfg (config.Load at complete.go:311) are all in scope at the advisory path; panelReg.Panel.ExpectedReviewers resolves (panel.go:54); the gate.go:257 `threshold > 0` third-defense clause is intact; ReviewerCountNote stays config-free so the leaf invariant holds. (3) Dependency shape: work_chunks encodes exactly 1:[] , 2:[1], 3:[1], 4:[2,3] — the 2‖ 3 parallel pair on the ADR anchor with Bead 4 on both, matching the plan text. Validator exits 0 with the single expected advisory (R=0.06 redundancy = the intentional disjoint cut).
findings: Status anchor VERIFIED: `grep -Eq '^- \*\*Status\*\*: Accepted'` matches ADR-0034/0037/0039 (all bolded `- **Status**: Accepted`). The round-1 R1 fix is correct — the bolding-tolerant regex is the right shape for this repo's header.
Domain anchor VERIFIED under canonical grep: `Domain\(s\).*\bcore\b.*\bworkflow\b` matches `- **Domain(s)**: core, workflow, ...` via /usr/bin/grep (BSD grep, GNU compatible 2.6.0-FreeBSD).

Bead-4 key_file_paths resolve: internal/complete/panel_advisory.go + panel_advisory_test.go both present; panelScanFn/panelTallyFn/panelAdvisoryOut seams exist (panel_advisory.go:28-30); panelGate returns *panel.Registration; a loaded config is in scope in the gate block (complete.go:311 gateCfg := config.Load(root)); panelReg.Panel.ExpectedReviewers resolves (panel.go:54).

ReviewerCountNote correctly does NOT exist yet (grep across internal/+cmd/ empty) so Bead 3 genuinely creates it; gate.go:257 `if f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0` intact; ApproveThreshold() int stays the single interpreter (panel.go:76) — leaf invariant preserved.

work_chunks depends_on shape encoded exactly as the plan text asserts: 1:[], 2:[1], 3:[1], 4:[2,3] (longest chain 3, 2‖ 3 parallel). `mindspec validate plan` exits 0; the lone WARN is decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.06 (the intentional package-disjoint cut the plan discloses), advisory only.

Advisory (not change-required): the Bead-1 Domain `\b...\b` anchor false-negatives under the Claude-Code interactive shell's `grep` function that routes to ugrep 7.5.0 (its \b+.* chaining differs); it passes under /usr/bin/grep. Implementer should run the content-anchor greps under POSIX/GNU grep (or `command grep`/`/usr/bin/grep`), not the ugrep-wrapped interactive shell, to avoid a spurious verification miss on a correctly-authored ADR-0040.
